name: Aman Resorts
philosophy: An inspired interpretation of Aman Resorts — the Singapore-founded ultra-luxury collection of 30+ properties known internally as 'the world's most discreet hotel brand'. Where Four Seasons whispers, Aman is nearly silent. Canvas is bone-white (#f4f0e8) or deep monsoon-black. Type is a restrained serif (custom Aman Serif / Garamond) at modest sizes — refusing the 80px display headlines that every other luxury brand has adopted. Photography is meditative travel-photography — no people, no smiles, no tables-with-wine; instead, single tatami rooms at dawn, fog over a Bhutanese valley, water-lily ponds at dusk. The brand voice is 'sanctuary' — a word Aman uses in every property description. The chrome is so reduced it nearly disappears, treating each property as a privately-owned retreat the visitor is being granted access to.

design_language.motion_signature: Meditative. Hero images cross-fade extremely slowly — 4–6 seconds between frames. No motion-graphics, no parallax. Hover states do nothing visible. The brand's signature is its absence of motion — silence as luxury.

trademark_signals: Bone-white canvas (#f4f0e8) with serif typography at modest sizes — refusing every luxury-brand convention of large display headlines
People-free photography — every Aman image is the empty room at dawn, the misty valley, the meditation pavilion before guests arrive — never staged with humans
'Aman' in lowercase or sentence-case serif lockup — the wordmark never shouts, never bolds, never goes all-caps
Property-name as koan — 'Amanjiwo', 'Amankora', 'Amanyangyun' — each name combines 'Aman' (Sanskrit: peace) with a regional word — the brand voice IS the naming
Long-form single-column property essays — 200+ words of contemplative description before any photo grid or booking CTA — luxury through patience
ai_slop_avoided: Don't add people to photography. Aman's people-free imagery IS the brand promise — 'this is your sanctuary, not a populated resort'
Don't increase display headline sizes. Type stays modest (24–40px max at hero); 80px display tier reads as off-brand 'modern luxury hotel'
Don't add a prominent CTA. Aman's CTAs are text-links or absent entirely; the brand never urges, never converts — it invites
Don't substitute the Aman serif with a sans-serif. The brand voice is contemplative-old-world; grotesk reads as Asian-boutique-hotel, not Aman
Don't show resort amenities as feature bullets. Property descriptions are atmospheric prose — pools, dining, spa all woven into a single contemplative narrative
